data.title: Centralized model ID configuration for DSO plugin
data.description:
## Context
DSO plugin maintainers face a manual find-and-replace burden whenever Anthropic releases a new Claude model version. Full model ID strings (e.g., claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-4-6-20260320) are hardcoded across 68 occurrences in 18 files — primarily eval configs, API-calling scripts, and the eval generator template. During previous model transitions, missed references caused eval configs to silently run against outdated models, producing results that didn't reflect the intended model tier. Updating to a new version requires touching every file individually, making missed references and version inconsistency an ongoing operational risk for the plugin maintainer.

## Success Criteria
1. dso-config.conf defines model.haiku, model.sonnet, and model.opus keys with full Anthropic model ID values; all three keys are required — if any key is absent, scripts that read model IDs exit with a non-zero status and descriptive error
2. All eval promptfooconfig.yaml files contain model IDs sourced from dso-config.conf via an update script; no hardcoded model ID strings remain in eval configs after the script runs
3. All scripts that call the Anthropic API directly (enrich-file-impact.sh, semantic-conflict-check.py) read model IDs from dso-config.conf via read-config.sh; if a model key is absent, the script exits with a non-zero status and descriptive error
4. The eval generator template (generate-skill-eval.sh) reads model IDs from dso-config.conf when scaffolding new eval configs
5. A grep for full model ID strings (matching claude-{haiku,sonnet,opus}-\d) across all files in plugins/dso/ and evals/ returns zero matches outside of dso-config.conf
6. A maintainer can update all model references across the entire plugin by editing dso-config.conf and running one script; git diff confirms changes are limited to dso-config.conf and promptfooconfig.yaml files, with no manual edits to any other file

## Scenario Analysis
- Eval YAML contains stale model ID after config update but before update script is run (high, runtime): Two-step process has an operational gap where eval files lag behind config
- Existing installations lack model keys; scripts fail silently (critical, deployment): Upgraded installations without model keys will pass empty strings to the API. SCs 1 and 3 address with required-key invariant
- Partial model key definitions cause inconsistent tier routing (high, configuration): SC1 addresses with all-three-required invariant
- Update script overwrites manually pinned model versions (medium, configuration): Script needs a skip-marker convention
- Key naming collision with consuming project config (high, configuration): model.* keys are generic; consuming projects could use them for other purposes
- Trailing whitespace in model ID values (medium, configuration): Manual edits may introduce whitespace the API rejects
- Update script not in runbook (medium, deployment): Script must be documented in upgrade/onboarding steps

## Dependencies
None. The "Project level config flags" epic (dso-0wi2) covers behavioral toggles (no-UI skip, hybrid PR mode) — no shared config plumbing or key namespace overlap.

## Approach
Add three model.haiku, model.sonnet, model.opus keys to the existing dso-config.conf. Scripts read values via read-config.sh. For eval YAML files (which can't source shell), provide an update script that reads the three model keys from dso-config.conf and rewrites the model ID values in all promptfooconfig.yaml files. The update script is run manually by maintainers after editing dso-config.conf.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 7
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
